comments.text: This is complex enough that it should probably be written in a preprint form (with lemmas, proofs, references, etc.). When doing so, I would recommend being particularly careful about the $o()$ notation, as there can be subtle issues regarding uniform dependence on parameters that can be obscured by this notation.
Okay, I will try to solve about 3 more then write a arxiv preprint for them

You have submitted several comments of this style yesterday - the length, style, and frequency of these comments suggests that these comments were written, at least in part, by an LLM. This is discouraged here. If you have a new idea about how to approach a problem then you are welcome to sketch it briefly here to see what others think. If you'd prefer to work out all the details in a formal proof yourself before sharing your idea, as Tao says, this would be better presented in a more traditional preprint form. I am not sure why you mention solving 3 more - the problems you have commented on are quite different and, assuming you have any genuine solutions (which, if they are generated by an LLM, I am very sceptical of, and encourage you to carefully check and rewrite all the steps yourself without AI assistance), they should appear in different papers, rather than a single paper solving several very different Erdős problems. Future comments of this length and style by yourself will not be shown on this site.
